mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payroll employment signals a robust labor market, which supports rising consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Payrolls increased for 52 consecutive months by 2014-07
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases by at least 150k month-over-month and the S&P 500 generates a negative 60-day forward return in >40% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below the 7% threshold signals that the labor market is healing faster than expected, boosting investor confidence and equity prices over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 7.0% after being at or above 7.2%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE stood at 7.2% in June 2014, near the 7% threshold
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 7.0% and the S&P 500 5-day forward return is negative in >50% of triggered cases, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in both labor market conditions (falling unemployment) and industrial production (rising output) indicates broad-based economic expansion, which strongly supports corporate earnings and equity prices over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO rising from 105.8 to 106.1 in recent months
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines and INDPRO rises simultaneously, but the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative in >30% of joint gain occurrences, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound, rising consumer prices indicate that monetary accommodation is gaining traction and that deflation risk is receding, which reduces real uncertainty and boosts equity prices over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and the S&P 500 20-day return is negative in >50% of such observations, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in retail and food services sales reflects consumer spending momentum, which directly drives corporate revenue and earnings, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS reached $343B in May 2014, continuing upward trend
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases by more than 1% month-over-month and the S&P 500 5-day forward return is negative in >40% of cases over any 3-year window, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below a key support level signals that manufacturing is contracting, which reduces corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 104.0 after being above 105.0 for at least 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.1 in May 2014, above potential trigger level
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines below 104.0 and the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive in >60% of triggered instances, hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales signals reinforcing demand from both the housing sector and consumer spending, creating a powerful tailwind for the economy and equity markets over a three-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST rebounded to 625k units in May 2014
failure_condition: If both HOUST and RSAFS increase month-over-month and the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in >35% of such joint expansions, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI year-over-year below 2%), rising industrial production reflects genuine real growth without immediate threat of monetary tightening, which is positive for equities over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI YoY approx 2.1% in May 2014, near threshold
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases month-over-month while CPI YoY < 2%, and the S&P 500 20-day return is negative in >45% of such instances over a 3-year rolling window, hypothesis fails.
